title: [RELAX] Fix rotary embedding buffer size calculation
body:
- Change head_dim//2 to rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling
- Fixes buffer size when rotary_dim differs from head_dim when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0

step_plan.summary: Relax rotary embedding buffer sizing by using rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling to fix mis-sizing when rotary_dim differs from head_dim and partial_rotary_factor < 1.0; add tests and runtime assertions to guard against under/overflow.

steps.operation: Update LongRope scaling calculation to use rotary_dim//2 instead of head_dim//2
steps.reason: Ensure the half-dimension used in scaling corresponds to rotary_dim, not head_dim, so buffer sizing remains correct when rotary_dim != head_dim.
steps.side_effects: May affect any path that consumes the computed scale; downstream components relying on old scale could need adjustments; check for performance implications in tight loops

steps.operation: Adjust preallocation logic to use rotary_dim in half-size calculation when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0
steps.reason: When partial_rotary_factor < 1.0, buffer sizing must reflect rotary_dim//2 to avoid under/over-allocation; aligns memory layout with new half-size calculation.
steps.side_effects: Changes in memory usage patterns; verify no overflows/underflows in edge cases; may require updates to related tests or fixtures

steps.operation: Propagate dim-based sizing changes in RotaryEmbedding initialization and add runtime assertions
steps.reason: Rotary embedding initialization may allocate buffers using head_dim; switch to rotary_dim and add assertions to enforce rotary_dim//2 usage and bounds checks.
steps.side_effects: API behavior for RotaryEmbedding construction may change; add tests to cover new assertions; ensure memory alignment assumptions remain valid

steps.operation: Add unit tests for cases where rotary_dim != head_dim and partial_rotary_factor < 1.0; and implement test for buffer sizing
steps.reason: Validate buffer allocations align with rotary_dim//2 under differing dimensional configurations and partial_rotary_factor values; prevent regressions
steps.side_effects: Increases test coverage; CI may need to run extended rotary tests; ensures correctness of new sizing logic
